description: Claire is Professor of Mineralogy and Radioactive Waste Management in the School of Earth Sciences at the University of Bristol. With an academic background in both geology and materials science and engineering, she has over 10 years of experience in researching radioactive waste degradation in geological environments. She has held research fellowships in both the UK and Japan and leads research efforts at the South West Nuclear Hub towards underpinning the disposal of radioactive wastes in subsurface facilities. Claire is an enthusiastic science communicator and has made numerous media and public appearances in relation to radioactive waste disposal and nuclear decommissioning. Appointed: January 2020 Term ends: January 2024 Reappointed: January 2024 Term ends: January 2028

details.body:
Claire is Professor of Mineralogy and Radioactive Waste Management in the School of Earth Sciences at the University of Bristol.

With an academic background in both geology and materials science and engineering, she has over 10 years of experience in researching radioactive waste degradation in geological environments. She has held research fellowships in both the UK and Japan and leads research efforts at the South West Nuclear Hub towards underpinning the disposal of radioactive wastes in subsurface facilities.

Claire is an enthusiastic science communicator and has made numerous media and public appearances in relation to radioactive waste disposal and nuclear decommissioning.

Appointed: January 2020
Term ends: January 2024

Reappointed: January 2024
Term ends: January 2028

Term ends: January 2028

title: Professor Claire Corkhill
